general_requirements.requirement: Proposed premises are not approved premises

still_needed: Statement confirming premises are not currently approved for PBS supply
Check PBS Approved Suppliers list for the address

general_requirements.requirement: Applicant has legal right to occupy proposed premises at all relevant times

requires_manual_verification: Fully executed lease or agreement to lease (signed by lessor and lessee)
OR title deed / sales contract if purchasing
If sub-lease: evidence head lease permits sub-leasing or head lessor consent
All applicants must be named in the lease
NOTE: Unsigned or non-binding leases are NOT sufficient

general_requirements.requirement: Council approval to use premises for pharmacy operation

still_needed: Property Planning Report showing suitable zoning with relevant schedules
NOTE: Landlord approval alone is NOT sufficient
NOTE: Lodged-but-undecided DA is NOT sufficient
requires_manual_verification: Council decision notice approving pharmacy use, OR
Letter from council confirming zoning permits pharmacy without DA, OR

general_requirements.requirement: Proposed premises accessible by the public

still_needed: Confirmation pharmacy is open to all public (not restricted to patients/staff)
No physical barriers preventing public access
requires_manual_verification: Floor plan showing public access points

general_requirements.requirement: Ready to trade within 6 months of ACPA recommendation

still_needed: Formal building/fit-out schedule with completion date
Quotes and/or contracts for works
requires_manual_verification: Dated photographs showing current premises status

general_requirements.requirement: Premises NOT directly accessible from within a supermarket

still_needed: Public access points to pharmacy clearly marked
Information about type of adjacent/adjoining shops
requires_manual_verification: Floor plan showing premises and adjacent shops

evidence_needed_from_engine: Verify public access door midpoints for 300m measurement
Verify prescriber roster and FTE calculation
Verify operating hours from centre management
Confirm ≥ 7 of the 8 FTE are medical practitioners

requirements.requirement: Premises in a large medical centre (single management, ≥70 hrs/week, ≥1 GP for 70+ hrs/week)

still_needed: - Single management arrangement
- Opening hours each day the centre operates
- Hours a prescribing GP is available for general practice consultations
requires_manual_verification: Statutory declaration from centre manager/owner confirming:
Floor plan of medical centre showing pharmacy location

requirements.requirement: No approved premises in the medical centre

requires_manual_verification: Statutory declaration from centre manager confirming no pharmacy in centre

requirements.requirement: ≥ 300m straight line from nearest approved premises (with exemptions for large SC/private hospital)

available_from_data: Scaled map or surveyor's report for distance measurement [AUTO-GENERATED from our data]
still_needed: If any pharmacy within 300m: evidence it's in a DIFFERENT large SC or private hospital
NOTE: This is a common failure point — ensure evidence covers ALL pharmacies within 300m

requirements.requirement: ≥ 8 FTE PBS prescribers (≥7 medical practitioners) for 2 months before application AND until hearing

still_needed: Summary of roster data
Start/finish times including breaks for each prescriber
Confirmation hours EXCLUDE: other centres, hospital duties, nursing homes, admin
Telehealth: only if GP physically present at centre AND patient is a centre patient
Minimum 304 hours/week total; max 38 hrs from non-medical PBS prescribers
NOTE: Authority WILL contact medical centre directly to verify
requires_manual_verification: Statutory declaration from centre owner/manager re: operating hours and each PBS prescriber's hours
Copies of rosters/timesheets for EVERY WEEK of 2 months pre-application

requirements.requirement: Reasonable attempts to match medical centre operating hours

still_needed: Statement that pharmacy will endeavour to match centre's operating hours
Intention to open outside normal hours if sufficient need exists
requires_manual_verification: Agreement between applicant and centre management regarding hours

Item 136.description: PBS prescriber count drops below 8 FTE (304 hrs/week)
Item 136.impact: Application MUST be withdrawn or will fail. This is the #1 reason Item 136 apps fail.
Item 136.monitoring: Weekly FTE tracking; locum arrangements; written GP commitments

Item 136.description: Medical practitioners (not total prescribers) drop below 7 FTE
Item 136.impact: Item 136(d) fails — at least 7 of 8 FTE must be medical practitioners
Item 136.monitoring: Track medical vs non-medical prescriber split weekly

Item 136.description: Medical centre reduces hours below 70 per week
Item 136.impact: No longer qualifies as 'large medical centre'
Item 136.monitoring: Monitor centre operating hours; watch for public holiday impacts

Item 136.description: GP availability for appointments drops below 70 hrs/week
Item 136.impact: No longer qualifies as 'large medical centre' — need ≥1 GP available 70+ hrs/week
Item 136.monitoring: Verify GP roster covers 70+ hrs per week

Item 136.description: Medical centre management structure changes (loses 'single management')
Item 136.impact: No longer qualifies as 'large medical centre'

Item 136.description: A new pharmacy opens within 300m (not in large SC or hospital)

all_relevant_times_window.description: Conditions must be met at BOTH: (1) application date, AND (2) ACPA hearing date
all_relevant_times_window.note: The ACPA will independently verify conditions at hearing. The Secretariat contacts medical centres directly to check hours.
what_changes: New pharmacy approvals in the area

mitigation_plan.action: Weekly FTE tracking; locum arrangements; written GP commitments
Track medical vs non-medical prescriber split weekly
Monitor centre operating hours; watch for public holiday impacts
Verify GP roster covers 70+ hrs per week

risk_report.recommendation: Critical risk. Multiple high-likelihood risks identified. Strongly consider delaying the application until conditions stabilise. Seek legal advice before proceeding.
